based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/xor-per-neuron-bias.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted v0.4 canonical bytes, per-neuron biases on h1/h2/y with bias_policy.mode=sgd). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

mutation.delta_vs_tolerance_multiplier: ~16162x (effective tolerance ~6.19e-11 at this magnitude under {atol: 1e-12, rtol: 1e-8})
what_was_NOT_mutated: updates[6].optimizer.factors[0].from ('backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value' — the provenance pointer Rule 8 resolves)
backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value (0.00618790217 — Rule 8 resolves the path and finds this UNMUTATED value, then compares against the mutated factor.value)
updates[6].gradient (0.00618790217 — kept at the original value; Rule 4 cascades because mutated factor multiplied through no longer equals stored gradient)

updates[7] (b_h2) and updates[8] (b_y) bias factors — unmutated; the same 0.00618790217 value appears in updates[0] (w_x1_h1) and updates[1] (w_x2_h1) as hidden_error_signal factor.value, NOT mutated here
mutation.rule_4_cascade_unavoidable: Rule 8 mutates a FACTOR value. Rule 4 multiplies factors to recompute gradient. Mutating the single bias factor's value perturbs the Rule-4 product by exactly the same delta (1e-6) because the per-neuron bias gradient is a one-factor product (gradient = factors[0].value). So Rule 4 ALSO fires on b_h1. This is structurally unavoidable for the 'mutate factor.value, leave from intact' fixture design — Rule 4 cannot pass when its inputs are wrong. Rule 8 is the targeted invariant; Rule 4 is the structural cascade. The reconciler distinguishes them via the field_path: Rule 8 reports field_path = the 'from' string ('backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value'), Rule 4 reports field_path = 'updates[6].gradient'.

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 8 failure naming parameter b_h1 with field_path = 'backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value' (the 'from' path). One Rule 4 failure on updates[6].gradient is the structural cascade.
reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 8: factor.value == value at the path indicated by factor.from
cascading_failures_observed: Rule 4 on updates[6].gradient (b_h1): the single-factor product (= factor.value) no longer matches stored gradient. Structural cascade — cannot be eliminated without making the fixture violate the 'single mutation' contract. Note the special case: for per-neuron bias updates the factor count is 1, so the cascade delta exactly equals the mutation delta (no other factor to multiply against).
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage) extended into the v0.4 per-neuron bias corner: proves the reconciler checks Rule 8 (provenance reference consistency — factor.value resolves to the value at factor.from) on bias updates by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch between the stored single-factor value and the resolved backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value. The Rule 4 cascade is acceptable and documented.
v0_4_note: For per-neuron bias updates the optimizer factor's `from` always points to a backward.<layer>_error_signals.<unit>.signal_value path. Rule 8 fires identically to the weight-update case; the only difference is the factor count (1 instead of 2).
